body:
# Feature request

## Summary

We should use `data-open` states across different components, and we could use `data-pressed` vs different state for triggers (tooltip may be a special case)

comments.body: @mnajdova Wouldn't this cause problems when composing different components, especially triggers?

i think it would cause some issues,

https://github.com/mui/base-ui/blob/master/packages/react/src/toggle/Toggle.tsx already uses data-pressed to indicate that the button is active. A toggle that opens a menu would be ambiguous - does `data-pressed` mean button is active or menu is open?

https://github.com/mui/base-ui/blob/master/packages/react/src/tooltip/trigger/TooltipTrigger.tsx opens on hover (not press), so it can't use `data-pressed`. Without `data-popup-open`, it has no way to indicate state.

https://github.com/mui/base-ui/blob/master/docs/src/app/(public)/(content)/react/components/menu/demos/submenu/css-modules/index.module.css#L31 use `&[data-popup-open]`

We intentionally had two attributes to separate click vs. hover triggered popups attached to the same element. A common use case is a Tooltip and a Menu attached to the same button. This way you can style each state (hovering into the tooltip) and clicking the Menu differently. e.g. `[data-pressed]` is darker, while `[data-popup-open]` matches `:hover`

labels.name: breaking change
labels.description: Introduces changes that are not backward compatible.

labels.name: scope: all components
labels.description: Widespread work has an impact on almost all components.

title: [all components] Consolidate the `data-open` and `data-presssed `states
